title: parse_duration drops the days (d) unit
body:
## Bug

`parse_duration` silently drops the **days** (`d`) unit. The README lists `d` as a
supported unit, but the parser ignores it and returns a wrong (too-small) result
instead of raising or counting it.

### Reproduction

```python
from duration_utils import parse_duration

parse_duration("1d")     # -> 0        (expected 86400)
parse_duration("2d4h")   # -> 14400    (expected 187200)
```

### Expected

`d` should be counted as 86400 seconds, so `parse_duration("1d") == 86400` and
`parse_duration("2d4h") == 187200`.

### Notes

The `d` character is already accepted by the token regex, so malformed-input
detection still passes — the value is just never added. A fix should add the
`d` unit and cover it with tests.

/bounty $50
